headline: Electronic Arts misses bookings estimates as 'Battlefield' engagement falls
summary: Videogame publisher Electronic Arts missed quarterly bookings estimates on Tuesday, weighed ‌down by a post-launch drop-off in ‌engagement for its "Battlefield" franchise and sluggishness in its mobile business. While "Battlefield 6" enjoyed a strong launch last year, the title has struggled with a slackening in player engagement, raising concerns about its ‌long-term live-service revenue ⁠potential. "Battlefield 6" has seen "significant attrition" in engagement with the "most-played" metrics on Xbox ⁠and PlayStation down from the third quarter, TD Cowen analysts said in a note last ​month.

headline: Grand Theft Auto VI Faces 25 Million Day-One Sales Expectations
